notes.text: WORKFLOW: actigraphy rest-activity profile. The consensus way to characterise a rest-activity record without assuming a sine shape (van Someren / Witting; Gonçalves et al. 2015). Step 1 — each group's subjects go into one Nonparametric RA node, which reports the four canonical variables per subject: interdaily stability IS (day-to-day consistency with the 24 h zeitgeber, →1 = stable), intradaily variability IV (fragmentation: transitions between rest and activity, →0 = consolidated), and M10/L5 (mean activity of the most-active 10 h and least-active 5 h) from which relative amplitude RA = (M10-L5)/(M10+L5) derives. Because the scalar metric ports report ONE VALUE PER Y INPUT, each port emits a whole column — that group's distribution. Step 2 — a Cosinor node on the same subjects adds the model-based view (amplitude, acrophase); combining nonparametric + model-based is the recommended pairing (Fossion et al. 2017). Step 3 — the two RA columns feed a Compare groups node, which treats each column as one group and tests whether rhythm robustness differs. The actogram shows a representative subject from each group so the numbers have a picture: the consolidated subject shows a solid daily block, the fragmented one is broken up. NOTE: this demo's data is already at the 1 h analysis epoch; logger data sampled finer (e.g. 30 s or 1 min) should be binned to the analysis epoch with a Bin node first, and `epochHours` set to match.
